nodes.label: Go Migration Rationale
nodes.rationale: Replaced Node.js with Go for native concurrency, lower CPU (29%→<15%), single-binary deployment (~5-10MB vs ~80MB), and float32 SIMD-capable FFT. IqExtractor was O(clients) sync in Node.js; goroutines give true parallelism per client.

nodes.label: IQ Compression Verdict: No Lossless Codec Worth Implementing
nodes.rationale: RTL-SDR IQ after NCO shift and Butterworth filter has near-random statistical structure. LPC prediction fails because samples follow a rotating phasor, not a slowly-varying waveform. ADPCM works by quantising amplitude differences (not prediction), achieving 4× lossy at negligible CPU. Best lossless (WavPack 1.43× WFM, FLAC 3.37× 48kHz) requires 15-150ms CPU/s per client — unacceptable on Raspberry Pi.
nodes.norm_label: iq compression verdict: no lossless codec worth implementing

nodes.label: Why Predictive Codecs Fail on RTL-SDR IQ
nodes.norm_label: why predictive codecs fail on rtl-sdr iq

nodes.label: FFT Deflate Selected as Default Codec
nodes.rationale: Delta-encoding exploits spectral coherence (adjacent FFT bins differ by small amounts), and zlib deflateRaw runs async via libuv thread pool. Achieves 7.5-10:1 lossless (from Uint8 stage) vs ADPCM ~8:1 lossy. ADPCM retained as fallback for deterministic latency on constrained servers.
nodes.norm_label: fft deflate selected as default codec
